user_query:
I’m building a shortlist of emerging, fresh voices from our latest open submissions. Using the included local files, please generate a reproducible, ranked shortlist and a brief report according to the criteria below.

Inputs:
- Metadata CSV: input/submissions.csv
- Writing samples: text files referenced by the sample_path column (under input/samples/)

Filtering criteria (keep only submissions that satisfy ALL):
1) Underrepresented voice: author_identity_tags contains at least one of these (case-sensitive match on the tag strings as they appear): {Indigenous, Black, Latinx, AAPI, Disabled, LGBTQ+, Refugee, First-Gen, Rural}.
2) Publication history: prior_publications_count <= 1.
3) Fresh genre: genre is one of {speculative, afrofuturism, cli-fi, hybrid, experimental, verse novel, magical realism}.
4) Thematic alignment: theme_keywords overlaps with at least one of {climate, migration, identity, memory}. (The theme_keywords field is a semicolon-separated list; match on exact keywords after trimming whitespace, case-insensitive.)

Text metric (computed from the sample_path file for each remaining submission):
- Tokenization: convert to lowercase; split on any non-letter character; discard empty tokens; consider tokens matching [a–z]+ only.
- total_words: count of tokens.
- unique_words: number of distinct tokens.
- unique_word_ratio: unique_words / total_words (as a floating-point value). Use this metric for ranking.

Ranking rules:
- Primary: unique_word_ratio descending.
- Tie-breaker 1: submission_date descending (more recent first; format is YYYY-MM-DD).
- Tie-breaker 2: prior_publications_count ascending.
- Tie-breaker 3: submission_id ascending (alphanumeric).

Deliverables (create these paths):
1) outputs/shortlist.csv — the top 5 ranked submissions after filtering (if fewer than 5 qualify, include all that qualify). Include columns: submission_id, author_name, author_identity_tags, genre, prior_publications_count, theme_keywords, total_words, unique_words, unique_word_ratio (rounded to 3 decimal places is fine).
2) outputs/snippets/ — for each row in outputs/shortlist.csv, write a file named <submission_id>_snippet.txt containing the first 200 characters from the original sample file (count characters in the original text, including spaces and punctuation; do not alter case or remove characters).
3) outputs/report.md — a brief summary that lists: (a) total submissions processed, (b) number that passed filtering, (c) the exact filter sets used (underrepresented tags, fresh genres, thematic set), (d) a bullet list of the shortlisted submission_ids in ranked order with their unique_word_ratio values.
4) outputs/REPRODUCE.txt — a single line containing the exact command you ran to generate outputs (e.g., a command-line invocation of a script you wrote). Please implement the logic via a script or command-line program and run it locally; do not compute manually.

Notes:
- All operations should be performed locally using the provided files. Do not fetch external data.
- The script/command must read input/submissions.csv, open each referenced input/samples/*.txt file as needed, perform the filtering and ranking above, and write all required outputs to the outputs/ paths.
- Ensure paths in outputs/shortlist.csv and outputs/snippets/ match the candidates selected by the specified rules so that results can be recomputed and cross-validated from the inputs.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
